<Meta title="Overview/Principles" />

# Lift's Guiding Principles

## Flexibility

We built Lift’s components as Web Components so that they are compatible with any front-end framework, not just Angular. Lift’s components are designed to work with one another, in whatever combination your application needs.

## Subtlety

Good design is 99% invisible. Lift is there to support your experience, not dominate it, so its visuals were designed to be clean and elegant rather than splashy and loud.

## Consistency

As our users move from one ATPCO application to another, their experience is easier and smoother when they can recognize common visual patterns. Lift’s flexibility means it can be used in lots of contexts while providing this common visual experience.

## Extensibility

Lift’s standard visual look was optimized for ATPCO’s web applications. However, the system is extensible, so that it can be used for visual variants (“themes”) for sites that have a unique visual look.

## Responsiveness and accessibility

ATPCO’s product user interfaces do not yet fully support responsive design or web accessibility, but we’ve built Lift with that future support in mind.
